channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NDDC Provides Relief to Ayakoro Residents Following Flooding
item.description: The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) don deliver aid materials to people of Ayakoro Community for Ogbia Local Government Area, Bayelsa State, after heavy floods don destroy farms, homes, and ...

item.title: Naira Gains Slightly Against Dollar As Market Gap Narrows
item.description: The Nigerian naira don record small improvement for parallel market on Tuesday, moving up from N1,400 per dollar wey e be last Thursday to N1,396 per dollar. This small rise show say market dey slight...

item.title: Massive Data Breach Exposes Sensitive LAPD Records
item.description: A significant data breach has compromised 7.7 terabytes of sensitive Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) records, including officer personnel files, Internal Affairs documents, and discovery material...

item.title: Police Crackdown in Sijimali: Multiple Injuries and Death of Cow Raise Human Rights Concerns in Odisha
item.description: A pre-dawn police operation in Kantamal, Odisha, on April 7, 2026, left several villagers injured, with one cow killed due to tear gas deployment. Police forces surrounded the village, cut off electri...

item.title: Astrological Predictions for Each Zodiac Sign on April 8, 2026
item.description: On April 8, 2026, astrologer Sally Brompton shares her daily horoscope insights for readers of The Globe and Mail. The piece opens with advice for those celebrating birthdays on this date, highlightin...

item.title: Improving Prison Conditions to Uphold Human Dignity
item.description: Kenya’s prison system has for a long time been facing serious challenges ranging from overcrowding, poor sanitation, and inadequate medical care to limited rehabilitation opportunities. Many facilitie...

item.title: K.C. Pharmaceuticals Recalls Over 3 Million Eye Drop Bottles Due to Sterility Concerns
item.description: K.C. Pharmaceuticals, a California-based company, has recalled more than 3.1 million bottles of lubricating eye drops due to inadequate sterility testing, raising concerns about potential eye infectio...

item.title: Five Japanese Deported for Links to Fraud Syndicate in Japan
item.description: Five Japanese nationals were deported to Tokyo on Tuesday morning after being arrested in joint operations by Philippine immigration authorities in Bulacan and Quezon. The Bureau of Immigration (BI) r...

item.title: US and Iran agree to two-week provisional ceasefire amid Trump threats
item.description: Political leaders and citizens in the United States breathed a collective sigh of relief after former President Donald Trump announced a provisional ceasefire with Iran, hours after threatening severe...

item.title: Mike Sonko Reaches Understanding with Baby James’ Family, Ensures Boy Stays Connected to Relatives
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko has finally reached a peaceful understanding with the family of Baby James, the young boy whose father was tragically killed by robbers last year. The custody disput...

item.title: Temporary Road Closures in Lagos for Bola Tinubu’s Project Events
item.description: The Lagos State Government don declare say some major roads go shut small-small as dem dey prepare for Bola Tinubu project openings. Ministry of Transportation don release notice say certain roads go ...

item.title: Council rejects former teacher's claim of intolerable working conditions
item.description: The Education Labour Relations Council has dismissed a constructive dismissal claim by Solly Rantho, a former teacher at Ilanga Secondary School, who resigned in July 2025. Rantho alleged that the Mpu...

item.title: US Pick-up Truck Manufacturers Dispute EU Safety Regulations
item.description: American car manufacturers are reportedly clashing with the European Union over the potential restriction of large pick-up trucks, like the Ford F-150, Chevrolet Silverado, and Ram 1500, from European...

item.title: Instagram to Discontinue End-to-End Encryption, Raising Privacy Concerns
item.description: Instagram has announced plans to discontinue its end-to-end encryption feature, a move that has ignited privacy concerns among users. The decision, reportedly made to combat illegal activities and com...

item.title: A Boy's Encounter with a Big Cat on Dartmoor: The Struggle to Be Believed
item.description: When I was 11, on a school trip to Dartmoor, my friends and I witnessed an astonishing sight: a large cat, bigger than a dog, moving silently through the mist. We were stunned, unable to grasp what we...

item.title: Truck Drivers Warn of Fuel Crisis Threatening Australian Industry's Future
item.description: Truck drivers and transport unions are raising alarm over the fuel crisis gripping Australia, with soaring diesel demand and rising fuel prices threatening to push the industry to a standstill. In a b...

item.title: ISRO Chief Highlights Importance of Mission Operations in Sustained Space Programmes
item.description: BENGALURU: While rocket launches often capture public attention, the real challenge for India's space programme lies in mission operations, according to ISRO chairman V Narayanan. Speaking at the Spac...

item.title: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra outlines current monetary policy stance and repo rate outlook
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) recently held its policy meeting where Governor Sanjay Malhotra addressed the nation regarding the central bank’s position on the repo rate and broader monetary policy....

item.title: Impact of Rapid Urban Growth on Abuja's Environment and Residents
item.description: Abuja, once admired for its green hills, forests, and planned urban spaces, dey face serious wahala as fast urbanization dey chop down trees and green belts. Between 2010 and 2023, forest cover don re...

item.title: US and Iran Agree to Temporary Ceasefire Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: In a significant development in the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran, both nations have agreed to a two-week ceasefire after intense military operations. Despite the announcement, missile stri...
